subject: Chemistry
solution:
Gold number ∝1protective power

It is defined as the minimum amount of protective colloid in milligrams which prevents a colour change from red to violet of 10 mL gold solution by the addition of 10 mL of 10 % NaCl Solution.

subject: Chemistry
options: Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mo> </mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi></mfrac></math> appear in the slope term.
solution:
According to Freundlich isotherm,


xm=k.p1n   {Taking log}

log⁡xm=log⁡k+1nlog⁡P

y=c+mx (Straight line equation)

So, intercept is log k and slope is=1n.

subject: Chemistry
options: It occurs because of vander Waal's forces.
More easily liquefiable gases are adsorbed readily.
Under high pressure it results into multi molecular layer on adsorbent surface.
Enthalpy of adsorption $\left(\Delta \mathrm{H}_{\text {adsorption }}\right)$ is low and positive.
solution: Enthalpy of adsorption regarding physissorption is not positive and it is negative.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Equation for Freundlich's adsorption isotherm is xm=KP1n

logxm=logK+1nlogP

Intercept logK=0.602=log4

K=4

Slope ⇒1n=1 so n=1

so xm=40.03=0.12=12×10-2g

Hence, the mass of the gas adsorbed per gram of the adsorbent is 12×10-2g

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The minimum concentration of an electrolyte in millimoles per litre required to cause precipitation of a sol in two hours is called coagulating value.

42.12 % means 100 ml solution contain 42.12 g of salt.moles of salt = 42.1258.5 = 0.72 moles = 720 milimoles

Since 100 ml required 720 milimoles

therefor 1 L will required 7200 milimoles.

It will coagulate in 10 hours so for 2 hours = 5×7200 = 14400 mili mol L-1

Note: NTA has either made mistake in question data or in options.

subject: Chemistry
options: Any amount of dispersed phase can be added to emulsion without destabilising it.
Mixing two oppositely charged sols in equal amount neutralises charges and stabilises colloids.
Presence of equal and similar charges on colloidal particles provides stability to the colloidal solution.
solution:
As equal & similar charge particle will repel each other, hence will never precipitate. So, the option D is correct.

Mixing two oppositely charged sols in equal amount neutralises charges and as a result colloids precipitate.

Only a certain amount of dispersed phase can be added to emulsion without destabilising it.

Brownian motion stabilises sols.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Colloid preparation by Hydrolysis reaction: (no change in oxidation number.)

FeCl3+3H2O→Fe(OH)3(sol)+3HCl

Gold sol is prepared by reduction reaction:

2AuCl3+3HCHO+3H2O→2Au(sol)+3HCOOH+6HCl

Preparation of sulphur sol:(oxidation)

SO2+2H2 S→3 S(sol)+2H2O

Preparation of colloid by double decomposition reaction:

As2O3+3H2 S→As2 S3(sol)+3H2O

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The graphical representation of logarithmic form of Freundilich adsorption isotherm is as follows,

The logarithmic form of Freundilich adsorption isotherm is

logxm=logk+1nlogP

1n=tan45°=1

logk=0.6020=log4

⇒K=4

∴  xm=K·P1/n

xm=40.4=1.6

xm=1.6=16×10-1

subject: Chemistry
solution: The motion of a liquid through a membrane under the influence of an applied electric field is known as electro-osmosis.

subject: Chemistry
options: Film of lyophilic sol is formed over lyophobic sol
Lyophilic sol is dispersed in lyophobic sol
Film of lyophobic sol is formed over lyophilic sol
solution: Film of lyophilic sol is formed over lyophobic sol as lyophilic sol acts as a protective colloid. Lyophobic solutions are unstable in nature. When a lyophilic sol is added to lyophobic sol, stability of lyophobic sol increases due to a protective film of lyophilic sol present over the lyophobic sol.

subject: Chemistry
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect.
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect.
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct.
solution:
Oil and water are not soluble in each other. since oils are non-polar organic compounds and water is a polar compound. So, they will form hydrophobic type of colloid.

Hydrophobic collides are unstable and required some stabilisers like proteins, gums, natural and synthetic soaps, etc. Excess of electrolyte can not act as stabilisers.

So, statement 1 is true but statement 2 is false.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Gold Number is the minimum amount of lyophilic colloide in miligrams (starch in given case) which prevents the flocculation of $10 \mathrm{ml}$ gold sol by the addition of $1 \mathrm{ml}$ of $10 \% \mathrm{NaCl}$ solution.
$\therefore$ Gold number $=$ miligram of starch $=0.025 \times 10^3 \mathrm{mg}$ $=25$

subject: Chemistry
solution:
(a) When gas is adsorbed on metal surface.
ΔH become less negative with progress of reaction because number of active sites decreases.
(b) Gas with greater value of critical temperature TC absorbed more. As TCNH3>TCN2 So NH3 absorbed more than N2.

Statement (c) is wrong because residual forces decrease as adsorption proceeds further.

Statement (d) is also wrong because temperature opposes extent of adsorption.

solution:
Cheese → liquid in solid
Pumice stone → gas in solid Hair cream → liquid in liquid
Cloud → liquid in gas

subject: Chemistry
solution:
(A) In physisorption, the enthalpy heat is low and lies between 20 - 40 kJ mol-1. In physisorption, multilayers of adsorbed particles are formed.

(B) In chemisorption, the enthalpy heat is high and lies within the range of 40 - 200 kJ mol-1.  In chemisorption, a monolayer of adsorbed particles is formed.

(C) N2g+3H2g⟶Fe(s)2NH3g

Heterogeneous catalysts occupy a different phase. Generally, heterogeneous catalysts are solid compounds that are added to liquid or gas reaction mixtures. Fe is used as a heterogeneous catalyst in the Haber process.

(D) In the process of adsorption chromatography, different compounds are adsorbed on the adsorbent to different degrees based on the absorptivity of the component. 

So, the correct matching is A-IV, B-II, C-III, D-I.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
In chromatography;

Benzaldehyde is a substance that is absorbed. It acts as an adsorbate.

Benzaldehyde:- Adsorbate

Alumina is a highly porous substance that adsorbs another substance. It acts as an adsorbent.

Alumina:- adsorbent

Acetonitrile is used as a mobile phase in chromatography.

Acetonitrile:- mobile phase

Stability These are very stable These are unstable and require traces of stabilizers

subject: Chemistry
solution: The Zymase enzyme converts glucose and fructose into ethanol and carbon dioxide. And the Invertase enzyme is used to convert sucrose or cane sugar into glucose and fructose. The enzyme diastase converts starch to maltose. The enzyme pepsin converts proteins to amino acids.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
According to Hardy's -schulz rule, greater the valency of the active ion or flocculating ion greater will be its coagulating power. S-2 is -ve charged colloid so cation having more +ve charge, more will be coagulating power.

Hence Al+3 having maximum coagulating power

subject: Chemistry
solution:
If FeCl3 is added to hot water, a positively charged sol, hydrated ferric oxide is formed due to adsorption of Fe3+ ions.

Fe2O3·xH2O/Fe3+

Positively charged.

Thus, When FeCl3â€‹ sol is treated with hot water, it forms Fe(OH)3​ forming Fe3+ ions(in excess amount) and these positive ions are adsorbed preferentially.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Coagulating value =Millimoles of electrolyteVolume of sol in litre

20 ml of 0.5 M NaCl contains NaCl=20×0.5=10 milli moles

200 ml of As2S3 required NaCl for the coagulation =10 milli moles

∴ 1000 ml of As2S3 required NaCl for the coagulation =10×1000/200=50 milli moles.
